sources_freshness: 2025-2026 — DayakDaily/TE articles reference Q4 2026 operations. Milestones span 2025-12 through 2026-Q4.
evidence_quality_rationale: Two independent news sources (DayakDaily + The Edge) corroborate specific milestones. RM58M contract award and ART unit arrivals are concrete, verifiable events. Minor concern: same DayakDaily URL reused for other projects but it's genuinely about KUTS.
trackability_rationale: Active news coverage in multiple outlets. Specific, dated milestones with completion status. ART vehicle arrivals are physically verifiable events. Could independently confirm via Sarawak Metro press releases.
notes: Flagship tracker project. Strongest evidence package. Consider adding Sarawak Metro official site or social media as live progress indicator. Add milestone for Blue Line station construction progress.

sources_freshness: Late 2025 — DayakDaily article on RM58M contract award. Milestones: 2025-12-15 (done), 2026-Q4 (pending).
evidence_quality_rationale: Single source, but it's specific and credible — contract award with exact value (RM58M) and named contractor (Linde EOX). Letter of Acceptance signed is a hard milestone. However, no follow-up construction progress source.
trackability_rationale: Only one source. No independent verification of plant relocation progress since contract award. The 2026-Q4 completion target has no progress reporting mechanism.
notes: This is a sub-component of KUTS (the hydrogen fuelling infrastructure). Strong candidate to MERGE as a sub-milestone under KUTS rather than standalone. If kept separate, needs construction progress updates and additional sources. RM58M is modest compared to the parent KUTS project.

sources_freshness: Undated — RECODA career page. Zero dated content specific to Baleh. Milestones: 2026 (pending), 'Ongoing' (vague).
evidence_quality_rationale: CRITICAL ISSUE: The only source is recoda.gov.my/career/ — a job listings page with ZERO project-specific content about Baleh HEP. This does not substantiate a RM10B dam project's status in any way. Milestones are vague ('2026', 'Ongoing') with no completion detail.
trackability_rationale: Untrackable with current data. A career page cannot verify dam construction progress. No percentage completion, no construction phase details, no contractor updates. For a RM10B Tier 1 project, this is a severe data gap.
notes: URGENT: Replace source with specific news articles about Baleh construction (Sarawak Energy press releases, Borneo Post, The Star coverage of impoundment progress). Add specific milestones: cofferdam completion, first turbine installation, impoundment date. Consider linking to Sarawak Energy's official project page. Score of 19 is high — project deserves proper tracking.

sources_freshness: 2025-2026 — DayakDaily articles on RM700B investment and PCDS 2030. Milestones span 2026-Q1 through 2050.
evidence_quality_rationale: Two DayakDaily news sources cover the policy announcement. The 2026-Q1 Sabah grid connection milestone is concrete and marked done. However, RM700B is a target investment figure for 2050 — not current spending. Policy framing is honest ('Active Policy' status).
trackability_rationale: Policy frameworks are inherently harder to track than construction projects. 2030/2050 targets cannot be verified now. The Sabah 50MW connection is verifiable but the bulk of milestones are aspirational. No link to actual SET-P policy document for detailed progress indicators.
notes: Important policy context but clarify its nature as a framework, not an executable project. Add link to official SET-P policy document (if published). Consider tracking periodic progress: renewable capacity mix percentage updates, annual investment figures. Low PRESS (Tier 3) is appropriate — policy has lower immediacy than physical projects.

sources_freshness: 2024-2025 — FULCRUM article on Sarawak's green hydrogen ambitions. Milestones: 2026, 2028, 2027+.
evidence_quality_rationale: FULCRUM is a reputable regional publication with substantive analysis. Status is honestly marked 'Planning / Early Stage.' However, single source for a Tier 1 multi-billion-dollar project is thin. All milestones are future-dated and none completed — this is expected for early stage but limits verification.
trackability_rationale: Early-stage project with no completed milestones. Future dates (2026-2028) mean nothing can be verified today. No SEDC Energy or Gentari primary sources. Would benefit from tracking JV agreements, FEED study awards, or land acquisition milestones rather than distant operational targets.
notes: High PRESS (Tier 1) warrants better sourcing. Add: SEDC Energy press releases, Samsung Engineering/Lotte Chemical announcements, Gentari partnership details. Add near-term milestones: FEED contract award, FID date, site preparation. The 2026 Rembus refuelling station milestone overlaps with Hydrogen Prod & Refuelling project — possible duplication.

sources_freshness: 2025 — DCD article on first off-taker announcement. Milestones: 2025-Q2 (done), 2026 (pending).
evidence_quality_rationale: Data Centre Dynamics (DCD) is a respected industry publication — quality source. USD130M Phase 1 is specific. Construction began in 2025-Q2 is a concrete milestone. However, single source and no construction progress verification since mid-2025.
trackability_rationale: Data centre projects typically have extensive industry coverage (DCD, Capacity Media, etc.) — surprising there's only 1 source. 'Under Construction' status cannot be independently verified with current data. No updates on whether 2026 operational target is on track.
notes: Add sources: TSG Group press releases, Global Telecommunications Group announcements, construction progress photos or reports. Data centre sector has abundant trade press coverage — leverage it. Consider adding milestones for: topping-out ceremony, power connection, first customer move-in.

sources_freshness: 2024-07 — MIDA presentation PDF. Milestones: 'Ongoing' and 2030 — neither dated specifically.
evidence_quality_rationale: Single source is a July 2024 MIDA presentation PDF — not a regularly updated source. Milestones are non-specific: 'Whole-of-society digital transformation' is a mission statement, not a trackable milestone. No completed milestones. This is a policy document, not a project with deliverables.
trackability_rationale: Effectively untrackable as a 'project.' Policy blueprints have no binary completion states. No KPIs, no interim targets, no progress indicators. A PDF presentation from 2024 cannot serve as an ongoing verification source.
notes: Poor fit for a project tracker. Candidate to MERGE into PCDS 2030 Framework as a sub-section on digital economy targets. If kept standalone: needs measurable KPIs (e.g., % government services digitised, number of tech startups, broadband penetration rates) with dated milestones and dashboard-style source links. As-is, it's a policy description, not a trackable project.

sources_freshness: 2026-02 — DayakDaily article shared with KUTS project. Milestones: 2026-Q1 (done), TBD (pending).
evidence_quality_rationale: SINGLE SOURCE SHARED with KUTS project — the same DayakDaily URL! GaN chip announcement is a secondary mention in an article primarily about ART. This is a data quality red flag. The milestone 'Premier announces GaN chip development success' needs a dedicated semiconductor-specific source.
trackability_rationale: The shared source limits independent verification. A GaN semiconductor project should have industry-specific coverage (electronics/semiconductor trade press). No financial figures, no technical specifications, no production timeline. 'Potential Indonesia collaboration' is too vague to track.
notes: URGENT: Replace shared DayakDaily URL with dedicated semiconductor sources. Search for: SMD Semiconductor press releases, Premier's GaN announcement transcript, semiconductor industry coverage. Add milestones: prototype completion, tape-out date, production volume targets, partnership MOUs signed. Project has potential but current data quality undermines it.

project_name: SCORE — Sarawak Corridor of Renewable Energy
sources_freshness: Ongoing/undated — RECODA.gov.my homepage. Milestones both marked 'Ongoing' with no completion criteria.
evidence_quality_rationale: CRITICAL ISSUE: RECODA homepage as sole source for a multi-billion economic corridor spanning multiple industrial parks and growth nodes. Milestones are circular: 'Ongoing' status with 'Ongoing' milestones proves nothing. No investor counts, no land allocation figures, no specific achievements.
trackability_rationale: Cannot track. SCORE is an umbrella programme — it needs to be decomposed into trackable sub-components (per industrial park, per growth node) with specific KPIs. A homepage URL cannot verify progress of individual parks and nodes.
notes: URGENT: SCORE should either be decomposed into sub-projects (Samalaju Industrial Park, Tanjung Manis Halal Hub, Mukah development, etc.) or populated with specific, dated milestones: number of investors secured, jobs created, land allocated, infrastructure completed. RECODA has annual reports and investment statistics — link those instead of homepage. For a Tier 1 PRESS 21 project, current data is embarrassingly thin.

sources_freshness: Unknown — dayakdaily.com homepage (no specific article). Milestones: 2026-Q1 (pending), before 2031 (pending).
evidence_quality_rationale: CRITICAL ISSUE: Source URL is literally 'https://dayakdaily.com' — the homepage, not a specific article about the Cancer Centre. This is essentially unsourced. The 'Pre-tender' status and Q1 2026 tender milestone cannot be verified against any published announcement.
trackability_rationale: Untrackable. No specific article URL means no way to verify: (a) PM's directive to JKR, (b) Q1 2026 tender timeline, (c) pre-tender status. Healthcare infrastructure projects typically have extensive government coverage — absence of proper sourcing is concerning.
notes: URGENT: Replace homepage URL with specific article(s) about the Sarawak Cancer Centre announcement. Search DayakDaily, Borneo Post, The Star, or Bernama for: PM's directive, JKR tender notice, budget allocation in State/Federal budgets. Add budget figure (currently '—'). For a healthy Tier 1 PRESS 20, current sourcing is a failure.

sources_freshness: 2026-02 — DayakDaily article shared with KUTS (about ART, not port). Milestone: 2026 (pending).
evidence_quality_rationale: CRITICAL ISSUE: The source URL is the same DayakDaily article about KUTS ART — completely unrelated to Bintulu Port. This is a sourcing error. The article does not mention port handover. 'Transitioning' status is unsubstantiated.
trackability_rationale: Untrackable with current source. Port handover from federal to state control is a major governance event that should have extensive coverage (parliamentary records, MOF statements, port authority announcements). Wrong source = zero evidence.
notes: URGENT: Replace source with Bintulu Port-specific articles. Search for: Bintulu Port Authority handover, Dewan Rakyat Hansard on port devolution, MA63 implementation updates, Sarawak government port takeover announcements. Add milestones: MOU signed, parliamentary approval, official handover ceremony date. Currently only 1 milestone — too few for a Tier 2 project.

sources_freshness: 2024 — FULCRUM article on Sarawak's low-carbon future. Milestones: 2023 (done), 2024 (done), Ongoing (carbon trading development).
evidence_quality_rationale: FULCRUM provides substantive analysis of Sarawak's carbon governance framework. Two completed milestones (ordinance enacted 2023, NR&E Bill 2024) are verifiable legislative events. However, single source and no link to actual legal gazette or government legislation portal.
trackability_rationale: Legislative/regulatory items can be verified via official gazettes and government legislation portals, but current source doesn't provide those links. Carbon trading market development is an ongoing process without clear completion criteria. Adding links to Sarawak State Legislative Assembly records would improve trackability.
notes: Reasonable regulatory tracker entry. Improvement: add links to the actual ordinance text (Sarawak Government Gazette), Sarawak Legislative Assembly Hansard for the NR&E Bill, and any carbon market framework announcements. Consider tracking: number of entities registered for carbon reporting, first carbon credit issuance, carbon trading platform launch.

sources_freshness: 2025-2026 — Business Events Sarawak page and Sarawak Tribune article on GLCs. Milestones: 'Achieved' (done), three 2030 targets (pending).
evidence_quality_rationale: Two sources — Business Events Sarawak is an official state agency site (good authority), Sarawak Tribune provides independent coverage. The 'Surpassed World Bank high-income threshold' milestone is a significant achieved outcome. However, the 2030 targets (GDP, income, jobs) are framework-level aspirations with no interim tracking.
trackability_rationale: Framework-level targets are inherently hard to track incrementally. RM282B GDP and 195K jobs are binary 2030 outcomes — no annual checkpoints. Would benefit from tracking interim indicators: current GDP, current median income, jobs created to-date, with annual update sources (Department of Statistics Malaysia, State Budget speeches).
notes: Essential meta-framework context for the entire tracker. But acknowledge it's a framework, not an executable project. Add: annual progress indicators with links to Department of Statistics data, State Budget annexures, or Premier's annual PCDS progress reports. Consider adding a 'last verified' date for GDP/income figures to signal data freshness.
